# easy-3dkit — Effects Reference

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/gen-effects-docs.mjs. Edit registry docs metadata, not this file. -->

A complete catalogue of every effect in the kit, grouped by its **master family**,
with the exact controls/props each exposes, what they do, and a ready-to-paste
usage snippet. Open the gallery (`pnpm dev`) and pick any effect from the
sidebar; its controls appear in the **leva panel** (top-right), and the **Docs**
button shows this same reference with a LIVE snippet of your current settings.

## How to use this kit

```bash
npm install easy-3dkit three @react-three/fiber @react-three/drei gsap
```

Three ways to drive any effect:

1. **In the gallery** — select it, tweak the leva controls live, then **Copy
   code** for the exact React file or **Copy JSON** for a portable config.
   Camera: the **Interact / View** toggle (bottom-center, or hold **Space**)
   switches between driving the *effect* with your cursor and *orbiting the
   camera*. Scroll-driven effects show a **scroll slider + Play** instead.
2. **In your own app** — import from `easy-3dkit` and pass props:

```tsx
import { Stage, CameraRig, InteractiveSurface, plasma } from 'easy-3dkit'

<Stage background={null}>
  <InteractiveSurface material={plasma} params={{ speed: 1.5 }} />
  <CameraRig />
</Stage>
```

3. **From a config** — paste a Copy JSON blob into `<KitElement config={...} />`
   (gallery layer) and it rebuilds the effect with no per-effect wiring.

### The 6 master families

| Family | What it is | How you use an effect |
|---|---|---|
| **InteractiveSurface** | A shader plane. Each effect = a `material` variant. | `<InteractiveSurface material={X} params={{...}} />` |
| **ParticleField** | GPU point cloud. | `<ParticleField {...props} />` |
| **InstancedGrid** | Thousands of instances, 1 draw call. Each effect = a `layout`. | `<InstancedGrid layout={X({...})} {...appearance} />` |
| **FloatingObject** | Spring/motion object wrappers. | `<ComponentName {...props} />` |
| **ScrollScene** | Scroll/time-driven scene effects. | `<ComponentName {...props} />` |
| **PostFX** | Full-frame post-processing. | `<PostFX {...props} />` (last child of Stage) |

### Viewing angle (CameraRig view)

Every scene's camera accepts a declarative viewpoint:

```tsx
<CameraRig view={{ azimuth: 30, elevation: 15, distance: 8 }} />
```

- `azimuth` — horizontal orbit angle in degrees (-180 to 180, 0 = front)
- `elevation` — vertical angle in degrees (-85 to 85, 0 = level)
- `distance` — camera distance from the target in world units
- `target` — orbit center, default `[0, 0, 0]`

In the gallery the **Camera** folder drives this live, and hand-orbiting in
View mode writes back into the sliders, so Copy code captures the exact shot
you framed. `onViewChange` gives you the same two-way binding in your app.

### Scroll modes and animation (ScrollAnimator)

Wrap ANY effect to make it scroll-aware and/or animated — all channels run
simultaneously and compose:

```tsx
<ScrollAnimator rotate={1} zoom={3} ease="easeInOut" entrance="rise" idle="bob">
  <InteractiveSurface material={heatHaze} />
</ScrollAnimator>
```

| Prop | What it does | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `rotate` | Full Y turns across the scroll range | 0 |
| `zoom` | World units dollied toward the camera | 0 |
| `lift` | World units risen (+Y) | 0 |
| `parallax` | Inverse lift + slight X sway; vary per layer for depth | 0 |
| `reveal` | Scale 0 to 1 over the first `reveal` fraction of scroll | 0 |
| `drift` | Deterministic x/y wander amplitude | 0 |
| `ease` | linear, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut, backOut, elasticOut | linear |
| `entrance` | One-shot intro on mount: rise, scaleIn, spinIn, dropIn | none |
| `entranceDuration` / `entranceDelay` | Entrance timing in seconds | 0.9 / 0 |
| `idle` | Endless ambient motion: bob, sway, pulse | none |
| `idleSpeed` / `idleAmplitude` | Idle timing and size | 1 / 0.15 |

Scroll channels need the page to be taller than the viewport (the gallery
fakes this with its scroll slider). The gallery's **Animation** folder drives
a ScrollAnimator around every effect, and Copy code / Copy JSON include it.

> **Controls note:** every numeric control shows `type · default (min-max)`.
> Colors are hex. For surface effects the control name is what you pass inside
> `params={{ }}`; for InstancedGrid layouts the layout options are passed to
> the factory and the rest are component props. Usage snippets show defaults —
> in the gallery, Copy code emits your CURRENT values instead.

---

## 1. InteractiveSurface

Shader effects on a plane. Each effect is a `material` variant: `<InteractiveSurface material={NAME} params={{ control: value }} />`. All react to the cursor and expose `uScroll`, so they also work in scroll scenes.

### Ripple Shader

A cursor-reactive GLSL plane. Great as a website hero backdrop.

Import `{ RippleShader }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `colorA` — color · #0a0a2a — Base color of the plane
- `colorB` — color · #4fc3ff — Color of the ripple crests
- `frequency` — number · 40 (5-120) — How many ripple rings fit across the plane
- `speed` — number · 2 (0-8) — How fast the rings travel outward
- `falloff` — number · 0.9 (0.1-1.5) — How quickly ripples fade with distance from the cursor
- `intensity` — number · 1 (0-3) — Overall strength of the ripple displacement

> Move the cursor over the plane to drive the ripples.

```tsx
<RippleShader
  colorA="#0a0a2a"
  colorB="#4fc3ff"
  frequency={40}
  speed={2}
  falloff={0.9}
  intensity={1}
/>
```

### Glassmorphism

Frosted translucent glass with an animated frost and cursor sheen.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, glassmorphism }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `tint` — color · #9ec5ff — Base glass color the frost noise modulates between its dark and bright shades
- `opacity` — number · 0.55 (0.1-1) — Baseline translucency of the panel before the cursor sheen adds extra alpha
- `blobScale` — number · 1.4 (0.3-4) — Spatial frequency of the layered frost noise, higher values give finer grain

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={glassmorphism}
  params={{ tint: '#9ec5ff', opacity: 0.55, blobScale: 1.4 }}
/>
```

### Thermal Vision

Thermal heatmap shading; the cursor adds a hot spot.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, thermalVision }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `intensity` — number · 1 (0.2-2.5) — Overall heat gain, pushing more of the surface toward the red and white-hot end of the ramp
- `noiseScale` — number · 3 (0.5-8) — Spatial frequency of the heat noise, higher values give smaller hot patches

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface material={thermalVision} params={{ intensity: 1, noiseScale: 3 }} />
```

### Iridescent

Oil-slick / soap-bubble shimmer that shifts hue with cursor + time.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, iridescent }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `bands` — number · 6 (1-16) — How many rainbow repetitions span the surface diagonal
- `shiftSpeed` — number · 0.3 (0-2) — How fast the hue spectrum drifts across the bands over time

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface material={iridescent} params={{ bands: 6, shiftSpeed: 0.3 }} />
```

### Frosted Glass

Heavy frosted-glass blur with fake refraction wobble.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, frostedGlass }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `tint` — color · #cfe6ff — Base panel color in the denser frost regions
- `highlight` — color · #ffffff — Color the frost shifts toward in brighter regions and along the rim sheen
- `frost` — number · 1.6 (0.2-5) — Spread of the 5-tap blur rosette, wider taps read as coarser grinding
- `refract` — number · 0.18 (0-1) — Strength of the noise-driven UV wobble that makes the frost swim like bent glass
- `opacity` — number · 0.55 (0-1) — Base panel opacity before frost density and rim glow add to it

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={frostedGlass}
  params={{ tint: '#cfe6ff', highlight: '#ffffff', frost: 1.6, refract: 0.18, opacity: 0.55 }}
/>
```

### Holographic Foil

Rainbow foil sheen with a sharp specular streak.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, holographicFoil }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `tint` — color · #ffffff — Multiplier color applied over the whole rainbow foil
- `hueScale` — number · 3 (0.5-8) — How many rainbow cycles fit across the fresnel sweep from face-on to grazing
- `streakSharp` — number · 24 (4-64) — Tightness of the anisotropic specular streak, higher collapses it into a thin band
- `grain` — number · 0.15 (0-0.6) — Amplitude of the micro-noise that breaks up the otherwise perfect rainbow bands

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={holographicFoil}
  params={{ tint: '#ffffff', hueScale: 3, streakSharp: 24, grain: 0.15 }}
/>
```

### Toon Cel

Cel-shaded quantized lighting with a fresnel outline rim.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, toonCel }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `base` — color · #4f9dde — Lit color used for the brightest diffuse bands
- `shadow` — color · #1b3a5c — Color the surface falls to in the unlit bands
- `steps` — number · 4 (2-8) — Number of flat brightness bands the diffuse light is quantized into
- `outline` — number · 2.5 (0.5-6) — Fresnel exponent for the silhouette darkening, higher pulls the ink line tighter to the edge

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={toonCel}
  params={{ base: '#4f9dde', shadow: '#1b3a5c', steps: 4, outline: 2.5 }}
/>
```

### Wireframe Morph

Grid lines morphing to solid fill over time/scroll.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, wireframeMorph }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `lineColor` — color · #39ff14 — Color of the grid lines and the flooded fill at full morph
- `fillColor` — color · #0a1f3c — Background color inside the grid cells
- `density` — number · 24 (4-80) — Number of grid cells across the surface
- `lineWidth` — number · 0.04 (0.005-0.2) — Base half-width of each grid line before the morph widens it into solid fill
- `morphSpeed` — number · 0.5 (0-3) — Rate of the time oscillation that swells lines toward full fill

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={wireframeMorph}
  params={{ lineColor: '#39ff14', fillColor: '#0a1f3c', density: 24, lineWidth: 0.04, morphSpeed: 0.5 }}
/>
```

### Moiré

Two rotated line gratings producing moire interference.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, moire }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `colorA` — color · #05060a — Color of the dark interference troughs
- `colorB` — color · #7df9ff — Color of the bright interference fringes
- `frequency` — number · 80 (10-200) — Line density of both gratings, higher packs more lines and finer beats
- `angle` — number · 0.08 (0-0.6) — Angular spread between the two gratings in radians, which sets the fringe spacing
- `sharpness` — number · 2.2 (0.5-6) — Contrast exponent on the grating product, higher gives crisper fringes

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={moire}
  params={{ colorA: '#05060a', colorB: '#7df9ff', frequency: 80, angle: 0.08, sharpness: 2.2 }}
/>
```

### Fractal Zoom

Animated escape-time fractal coloring; cursor pans.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, fractalZoom }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `iterations` — number · 96 (16-512) — Maximum escape-time iterations, higher resolves finer boundary detail at more cost
- `zoomSpeed` — number · 0.18 (0-1) — Rate of the exponential dive into the fractal over time
- `julia` — number · 0 (0-1) — Blends the seed from Mandelbrot at 0 to a fixed-c Julia set at 1
- `inside` — color · #05060f — Flat tint for interior points that never escape
- `palette` — color · #3fa9ff — Color whose channels set the base hue offset, saturation and brightness of the escape coloring

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={fractalZoom}
  params={{ iterations: 96, zoomSpeed: 0.18, julia: 0, inside: '#05060f', palette: '#3fa9ff' }}
/>
```

### Liquid Blob

Metaball blobs via smooth-min distance fields.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, liquidBlob }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 5 (1-12) — How many orbiting metaballs contribute to the field
- `smooth` — number · 0.35 (0.05-1) — Smooth-min blend width that controls how gooily blobs fuse together
- `speed` — number · 0.6 (0-3) — How fast the metaballs orbit their paths
- `colorA` — color · #0a1fff — Palette endpoint the blob body mixes from
- `colorB` — color · #ff2d95 — Palette endpoint the blob body mixes toward

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={liquidBlob}
  params={{ count: 5, smooth: 0.35, speed: 0.6, colorA: '#0a1fff', colorB: '#ff2d95' }}
/>
```

### Brushed Metal

Anisotropic brushed-metal highlight streaks.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, brushedMetal }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `base` — color · #9aa2ad — Underlying metal color the scratches modulate
- `sheen` — color · #f2f5fa — Highlight color used for the specular band and fresnel edge lift
- `grain` — number · 220 (40-600) — Scratch frequency along the grain axis, higher gives finer brushing
- `aniso` — number · 0.85 (0-1) — Dials from isotropic matte metal at 0 to fully stretched parallel streaks at 1
- `contrast` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How strongly the scratches darken and lighten the base, from flat to full streak spread

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={brushedMetal}
  params={{ base: '#9aa2ad', sheen: '#f2f5fa', grain: 220, aniso: 0.85, contrast: 0.5 }}
/>
```

### Neon Line Art

Emissive glowing line-art grid that pulses.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, neonLineArt }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `lineColor` — color · #00ffd5 — Emissive color of the glowing grid tubes
- `bgColor` — color · #04060a — Near-black backdrop the lines are added over
- `density` — number · 14 (4-40) — Number of grid cells across the surface
- `thickness` — number · 0.04 (0.005-0.2) — Half-width of each line in cell space
- `pulse` — number · 1 (0-3) — Global brightness multiplier on the travelling glow wave

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={neonLineArt}
  params={{ lineColor: '#00ffd5', bgColor: '#04060a', density: 14, thickness: 0.04, pulse: 1 }}
/>
```

### Bioluminescent

Dark surface with glowing organic cells that breathe.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, bioluminescent }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `glow` — color · #27f5c8 — Emissive color of the pulsing filament cells
- `base` — color · #020308 — Near-black surface color the glow is added onto
- `scale` — number · 4 (1-12) — Cell density of the domain-warped noise field, higher gives smaller filaments
- `breath` — number · 0.6 (0.05-2) — Speed of the out-of-phase brightening and dimming of each cell region
- `intensity` — number · 1.4 (0.2-4) — Gain on the emissive glow added over the base

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={bioluminescent}
  params={{ glow: '#27f5c8', base: '#020308', scale: 4, breath: 0.6, intensity: 1.4 }}
/>
```

### X-Ray Ghost

Fresnel translucent edge-glow ghostly shading.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, xrayGhost }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `rimColor` — color · #9fe8ff — Luminous color of the grazing-angle fresnel rim
- `coreColor` — color · #0a1830 — Faint color of the hollow body away from the edges
- `rimPower` — number · 2.6 (0.5-8) — Fresnel exponent that sets how tightly the glow hugs the silhouette
- `opacity` — number · 0.55 (0-1) — Overall alpha scale, with the core staying near-invisible and the rim solid

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={xrayGhost}
  params={{ rimColor: '#9fe8ff', coreColor: '#0a1830', rimPower: 2.6, opacity: 0.55 }}
/>
```

### Rain Streaks

Vertical rain streaks with droplets running down glass.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, rainStreaks }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `glassColor` — color · #0a1822 — Base color of the wet glass pane
- `streakColor` — color · #aaccdd — Color of the droplet beads, trails and condensation haze
- `density` — number · 40 (8-120) — Number of vertical rain columns across the surface
- `speed` — number · 0.6 (0.1-2) — Multiplier on how fast droplets fall down their columns
- `blur` — number · 0.35 (0.05-1) — Lateral softness of each streak, wider reads as more out-of-focus refraction

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={rainStreaks}
  params={{ glassColor: '#0a1822', streakColor: '#aaccdd', density: 40, speed: 0.6, blur: 0.35 }}
/>
```

### Scanlines

CRT scanlines with chromatic offset and flicker.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, scanlines }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `tint` — color · #39ff66 — Phosphor color the monochrome image is tinted toward
- `dark` — color · #02060a — Frame color the vignette fades to at the edges
- `lineFreq` — number · 220 (40-600) — Frequency of the horizontal scanline darkening, higher gives finer rows
- `aberration` — number · 0.004 (0-0.02) — RGB split distance that grows toward the tube edges
- `flicker` — number · 0.08 (0-0.4) — Depth of the global brightness wobble

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={scanlines}
  params={{ tint: '#39ff66', dark: '#02060a', lineFreq: 220, aberration: 0.004, flicker: 0.08 }}
/>
```

### Dither 8-bit

Ordered-dither posterized retro shading.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, dither8bit }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `dark` — color · #0b1d3a — Palette endpoint for the lowest quantized levels
- `light` — color · #7fe3ff — Palette endpoint for the brightest levels, with a slow built-in hue shimmer
- `levels` — number · 4 (2-12) — Number of brightness steps the gradient is posterized into
- `pixel` — number · 96 (16-256) — Logical pixel-grid resolution, lower gives chunkier retro blocks

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={dither8bit}
  params={{ dark: '#0b1d3a', light: '#7fe3ff', levels: 4, pixel: 96 }}
/>
```

### Kinetic Type

Distortion field warping a procedural stripe pattern.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, kineticType }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `ink` — color · #0a0a0a — Dark stripe color
- `paper` — color · #f5f2e8 — Light background color between the stripes
- `freq` — number · 24 (4-60) — Diagonal stripe density across the surface
- `warp` — number · 0.35 (0-1) — Amplitude of the flowing noise that bends the stripe phase
- `pull` — number · 0.6 (0-2) — Strength of the cursor lens that pushes stripes away from the pointer

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={kineticType}
  params={{ ink: '#0a0a0a', paper: '#f5f2e8', freq: 24, warp: 0.35, pull: 0.6 }}
/>
```

### Plasma

Classic demoscene plasma via summed sines.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, plasma }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `scale` — number · 8 (2-24) — Spatial frequency of the summed sine waves, higher packs more swirls into the surface
- `speed` — number · 0.6 (0-3) — How fast the plasma interference pattern flows
- `colorA` — color · #ff2d75 — First brand tint the rainbow output is biased toward on the wave crests
- `colorB` — color · #1fa2ff — Second brand tint mixed in along the wave fold

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={plasma}
  params={{ scale: 8, speed: 0.6, colorA: '#ff2d75', colorB: '#1fa2ff' }}
/>
```

### Voronoi Cells

Animated voronoi cells lit near the cursor.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, voronoiCells }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `scale` — number · 6 (2-20) — Number of Voronoi cells across the surface
- `speed` — number · 0.6 (0-3) — How fast the cell sites wander on their per-cell phases
- `lightRadius` — number · 0.5 (0.05-1.5) — Reach of the cursor spotlight that brightens nearby cells
- `cellColor` — color · #33e0ff — Color of the cell interiors, hue-jittered per cell
- `edgeColor` — color · #0a0f24 — Dark color of the borders where neighbouring cells meet

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={voronoiCells}
  params={{ scale: 6, speed: 0.6, lightRadius: 0.5, cellColor: '#33e0ff', edgeColor: '#0a0f24' }}
/>
```

### Heat Haze

Refractive heat-shimmer over a gradient backdrop.

Import `{ InteractiveSurface, heatHaze }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `colorTop` — color · #1b2a4a — Gradient color at the cool top of the surface
- `colorBottom` — color · #ff7a3c — Gradient color at the hot bottom where the haze pools
- `strength` — number · 0.04 (0-0.2) — How far the shimmer displaces the background gradient
- `speed` — number · 1.2 (0-4) — How fast the heat plumes rise
- `scale` — number · 5 (1-16) — Spatial frequency of the distortion noise, higher gives finer ripples

```tsx
<InteractiveSurface
  material={heatHaze}
  params={{ colorTop: '#1b2a4a', colorBottom: '#ff7a3c', strength: 0.04, speed: 1.2, scale: 5 }}
/>
```

---

## 2. ParticleField

A configurable GPU point cloud: `<ParticleField {...props} />`.

### Particle Field

A configurable GPU point cloud. Ambient atmosphere for any scene.

Import `{ ParticleField }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 4000 (200-30000) — How many points are in the cloud
- `radius` — number · 4 (1-12) — Overall size of the cloud in world units
- `distribution` — select · sphere · sphere | cube | disc | shell — Shape the points fill: solid sphere, cube, flat disc, or hollow shell
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Point color
- `size` — number · 0.04 (0.005-0.3) — Size of each point
- `opacity` — number · 1 (0.05-1) — Point transparency
- `glow` — boolean · true — Additive blending so overlapping points brighten
- `sizeAttenuation` — boolean · true — Points shrink with distance from the camera
- `speed` — number · 0.05 (0-1) — How fast the cloud tumbles
- `tumble` — number · 0.4 (0-2) — How much the tumble axis wanders

```tsx
<ParticleField
  count={4000}
  radius={4}
  distribution="sphere"
  color="#5fa8ff"
  size={0.04}
  opacity={1}
  glow={true}
  sizeAttenuation={true}
  speed={0.05}
  tumble={0.4}
/>
```

---

## 3. InstancedGrid

Thousands of instances in a single draw call. Each effect is a `layout` factory: `<InstancedGrid layout={NAME({...})} {...appearance} />`.

### Orbit Layout

Hundreds of instances on nested orbiting shells in a single draw call. Cursor nudges the cloud.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, orbitLayout }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 600 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `shells` — number · 5 (1-12) — How many nested orbit shells the instances spread across
- `radius` — number · 1.5 (0.5-5) — Radius of the innermost shell
- `spacing` — number · 0.6 (0.1-2) — Gap between consecutive shells
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Orbit speed
- `cursorPush` — number · 0.5 (0-3) — How strongly the cursor repels nearby instances
- `shape` — select · box · box | sphere | icosahedron | octahedron | tetrahedron | cone — Primitive geometry used for every instance
- `instanceSize` — number · 0.12 (0.02-0.5) — Size of each instance
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `emissive` — color · #000000 — Self-illumination color
- `emissiveIntensity` — number · 0 (0-5) — Strength of the self-illumination
- `wireframe` — boolean · false — Render instances as wireframe

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={orbitLayout({ count: 600, shells: 5, radius: 1.5, spacing: 0.6, speed: 1, cursorPush: 0.5 })}
  shape="box"
  instanceSize={0.12}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  roughness={0.3}
  metalness={0.5}
  emissive="#000000"
  emissiveIntensity={0}
  wireframe={false}
/>
```

### Infinite Tunnel

Receding rings forming a tunnel scrolling toward camera.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, tunnelLayout }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={tunnelLayout({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Isometric Stack

Staggered cube grid with a Z-height ripple wave.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, isometricStack }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

> Instance count is derived from the layout grid (cols * cols).

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={isometricStack()}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Voxel Sphere

Instances voxelizing a sphere surface; cursor displaces.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, voxelSphere }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={voxelSphere({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Voronoi Shatter

Shards exploding outward and settling back, looping.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, voronoiShatter }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={voronoiShatter({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Gear Field

Grid of instances spinning like interlocking gears.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, gearField }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={gearField({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Kinetic Ring

Instances on a tumbling ring facing outward.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, kineticRing }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={kineticRing({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Origami Fold

Rows folding/unfolding accordion-style.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, origamiFold }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={origamiFold({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Wave Grid

Flat grid with a traveling sine wave; cursor bumps it.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, waveGrid }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

> Instance count is derived from the layout grid (cols * cols).

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={waveGrid()}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Galaxy Spiral

Instances on logarithmic spiral arms rotating differentially.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, galaxySpiral }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={galaxySpiral({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

### Cube Swarm

Drifting bounded swarm with index-phase cohesion.

Import `{ InstancedGrid, cubeSwarm }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `count` — number · 800 (100-5000) — Total number of instances (one draw call)
- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Instance color
- `metalness` — number · 0.5 (0-1) — How metallic instances respond to light
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)

```tsx
<InstancedGrid
  layout={cubeSwarm({ count: 800 })}
  color="#5fa8ff"
  metalness={0.5}
  roughness={0.3}
/>
```

---

## 4. FloatingObject

Spring/motion object wrappers: `<ComponentName {...props} />`.

### Floating Object

Idle float + spin with a hover pop. Every aspect configurable.

Import `{ FloatingObject }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `shape` — select · torusKnot · box | sphere | torus | torusKnot | cone | cylinder | icosahedron | dodecahedron | octahedron | tetrahedron — Which primitive geometry to float
- `detail` — number · 1 (0-4) — Geometry subdivision / segment count
- `color` — color · #ff7a59 — Surface color
- `roughness` — number · 0.3 (0-1) — Microsurface roughness (0 = mirror, 1 = matte)
- `metalness` — number · 0.4 (0-1) — How metallic the surface responds to light
- `emissive` — color · #000000 — Self-illumination color
- `emissiveIntensity` — number · 0 (0-5) — Strength of the self-illumination
- `wireframe` — boolean · false — Render as wireframe
- `opacity` — number · 1 (0-1) — Surface transparency
- `flatShading` — boolean · false — Faceted (low-poly) shading instead of smooth normals
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Speed of the idle float cycle
- `amplitude` — number · 0.3 (0-1.5) — How far the object bobs up and down
- `spin` — number · 1 (0-4) — Continuous Y rotation speed
- `hoverScale` — number · 1.15 (1-2.5) — Scale multiplier while the cursor hovers the object
- `position` — vector · [0, 0, 0] — Base position in world units
- `rotation` — vector · [0, 0, 0] — Base rotation in radians
- `scale` — number · 1 (0.1-4) — Uniform scale multiplier

```tsx
<FloatingObject
  shape="torusKnot"
  detail={1}
  color="#ff7a59"
  roughness={0.3}
  metalness={0.4}
  emissive="#000000"
  emissiveIntensity={0}
  wireframe={false}
  opacity={1}
  flatShading={false}
  speed={1}
  amplitude={0.3}
  spin={1}
  hoverScale={1.15}
  position={[0, 0, 0]}
  rotation={[0, 0, 0]}
  scale={1}
/>
```

### Card Flip

3D card that flips on hover with an idle tilt.

Import `{ CardFlip }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

> Hover the card to flip it.

```tsx
<CardFlip color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Magnetic Group

Meshes that drift toward the cursor then ease back.

Import `{ MagneticGroup }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<MagneticGroup color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Squash Stretch

Bouncing sphere with squash-and-stretch deformation.

Import `{ SquashStretch }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<SquashStretch color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Elastic Jiggle

Mesh that jiggles elastically when the cursor moves.

Import `{ ElasticJiggle }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<ElasticJiggle color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Path Spline

Mesh following a looping lissajous path with a ghost trail.

Import `{ PathSpline }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<PathSpline color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Morph Shape

Mesh morphing between sphere and box over time.

Import `{ MorphShape }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<MorphShape color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

---

## 5. ScrollScene

Scroll/time-driven scene effects: `<ComponentName {...props} />`. Preview their scroll range with the gallery scroll slider.

### Scroll Scene

Binds page scroll to a 3D transform. (Scroll the page to see it drive.)

Import `{ ScrollScene, FloatingObject }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `rotations` — number · 1 (0-4) — Full Y rotations across the whole scroll range
- `zTravel` — number · 4 (0-10) — How far the group dollies toward the camera across the range

> Wrap any children; they stay scroll-ignorant. For more channels (lift, parallax, reveal, drift) plus entrance and idle animation, use ScrollAnimator instead.

```tsx
<ScrollScene rotations={1} zTravel={4}>
  <FloatingObject />
</ScrollScene>
```

### Exploded View

Parts separating along Y as time rises (exploded view).

Import `{ ExplodedView }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<ExplodedView color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Parallax Layers

Depth-layered planes that parallax with the cursor.

Import `{ ParallaxLayers }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<ParallaxLayers color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Ocean Plane

Subdivided plane with Gerstner sine-wave displacement.

Import `{ OceanPlane }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<OceanPlane color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Portal Ring

Glowing torus portal with a swirling interior + particles.

Import `{ PortalRing }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<PortalRing color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Camera Flythrough

Gates/rings drifting past the camera on a loop.

Import `{ CameraFlythrough }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<CameraFlythrough color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

### Popup Fold

Flat panels folding up into a 3D pop-up arrangement.

Import `{ PopupFold }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `color` — color · #5fa8ff — Primary color of the meshes
- `speed` — number · 1 (0-4) — Animation speed multiplier

```tsx
<PopupFold color="#5fa8ff" speed={1} />
```

---

## 6. PostFX

Full-frame post-processing: `<PostFX {...props} />` as the LAST child of Stage. Requires the optional peer `@react-three/postprocessing`.

### Post FX

Composable bloom / vignette / grain. Add as the last Stage child.

Import `{ PostFX }` from `easy-3dkit/postprocessing`.

Import `{ FloatingObject, ParticleField }` from `easy-3dkit`.

**Controls:**

- `bloom` — number · 1.2 (0-3) — Bloom (glow) strength on bright areas
- `bloomThreshold` — number · 0.4 (0-1) — Luminance above which pixels start to bloom
- `vignette` — number · 0.5 (0-1.5) — Darkening toward the screen edges
- `noise` — number · 0 (0-0.5) — Animated film-grain amount

> Requires the optional peer @react-three/postprocessing. Add as the LAST child of Stage.

```tsx
<FloatingObject color="#88ddff" />
<ParticleField count={1500} radius={5} />
<PostFX
  bloom={1.2}
  bloomThreshold={0.4}
  vignette={0.5}
  noise={0}
/>
```
---

_Generated from the source of truth (each module's own controls/props/docs) by
`pnpm docs:effects`. 21 surface effects · 11 instance layouts ·
49 entries across 6 families._
